start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text: [i]The comms panel crackles. Not the usual hiss of empty channels—something else. A deliberate signal, encrypted, threading through frequencies the ship shouldn't be monitoring.[/i]

waffle_alert.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_alert.text: Unknown transmission. Source: [b]redacted[/b]. Encryption: custom. Not Guild. Not Compact. Someone built this specifically to reach us.

signal_incoming.speaker: SYSTEM
signal_incoming.text: [i]Static blooms, then clears just enough to carry a voice—low, careful, masked beneath layers of noise.[/i]

mentor_greeting.text: [i][Static][/i] —listening? Good. Don't respond yet. Just hear this: [b]the girl you rescued from Site K-9 is more valuable than you know.[/b]

mentor_warning.text: Corporate interest in Avyanna isn't coincidence. They know what she's carrying. They'll come for her. Not now—but soon. When they do, it won't be a raid. It'll be [b]legal extraction[/b].

elia_listens.speaker: Elia
elia_listens.text: [i]Jaw tight, hand near her blade.[/i] Who the hell is this?

mentor_deflect.text: Someone who wants her to survive. Someone who's been watching the Kennel for fifteen years. [i][Static surge][/i] —doesn't matter who I am. What matters is [b]she's a target[/b].

mentor_reveal_shard.text: The shard in her spine? Pre-Synthetic. Primordial. The Debtors want it back. Aurum Extraction wants compensation. The Compact will call it [b]controlled substance trafficking[/b].

mentor_lattice_hint.text: But the shard isn't what makes her dangerous. It's what it woke up. [i][Static crackle][/i] Her resonance with the Lattice—that's why they took her in the first place. That's why [b]extraction[/b] wasn't random.

bee_agitation_early.speaker: SYSTEM
bee_agitation_early.text: {BEE:: lattice resonance referenced — unknown source | ELEVATED | pattern matches restricted K-9 documentation}

mentor_preextraction.text: Before they pulled her out of wherever she was born, she was [b]flagged[/b]. A Lattice-sensitive child, manifesting at age three. The Debtors bid on her like cattle at market. Aurum won.

mentor_targeted.text: She wasn't rescued from K-9, not really. She was [b]moved[/b]. From one cage to a smaller one. The shard was their leash. And now that she's loose— [i][static hiss][/i] —they want their investment back.

player_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
player_choice.text: [i]The voice waits. Elia looks at you. The choice is yours.[/i]

choices.text: [Trust] Ask them what we should do.
[Demand Answers] Who are you? Why should we believe this?
[Cut Transmission] This is manipulation. End it now.
[Trace Signal] Keep them talking. Waffle—lock onto that signal.

trust_response.text: [i]A pause. The static shifts—something like relief.[/i] Smart. Keep her off station registries. Stay mobile. Don't let her touch Compact medical scanners. And when the time comes... trust the shard.

trust_followup.text: I'll contact you again when it's safe. Until then—[b]keep her hidden. Keep her learning.[/b]

demand_response.text: [i]A sound that might be a laugh, distorted through static.[/i] Fair question. I'm someone who failed to get her out sooner. Someone who's trying to make that right.

demand_followup.text: Believe me or don't. But [b]watch the corporate feeds[/b]. Watch for Aurum Extraction filing debt recovery claims. You'll see I'm right.

cut_response.speaker: Elia
cut_response.text: [i]She kills the transmission with a single gesture.[/i] If they wanted to help, they'd show their face. We don't trust shadows.

cut_followup.speaker: Waffle.bat
cut_followup.text: Signal terminated. Source remains unknown. [i][Beat.][/i] Recommendation: Monitor corporate feeds anyway. Just in case.

trace_attempt.speaker: Waffle.bat
trace_attempt.text: Attempting signal lock... Encryption is layered, rotating. I need more time. [b]Keep them talking.[/b]

trace_success.speaker: Waffle.bat
trace_success.text: Partial lock acquired. Signal origin: [b]Crucible Station, Sublevel 9[/b]. Residential block. Registered to a dead woman—expired thirty years ago. Whoever this is, they've been a ghost a long time.

trace_failure.speaker: Waffle.bat
trace_failure.text: Lost it. Encryption rotated faster than I could pin. All I caught was a region tag—somewhere in the Crucible cluster. Could be anyone.

trace_reaction.text: [i]The voice turns colder.[/i] Clever. Doesn't matter. You won't find me unless I want to be found. But I'll forgive the attempt—because you're going to need what I say next.

mentor_crew_warning.text: [i][Static drops to a whisper.][/i] One more thing. Someone on your crew isn't what they seem. I don't know who. The signal noise makes it hard to be sure. But [b]someone close to you has a second loyalty.[/b]

mentor_crew_warning_2.text: Could be debt. Could be ideology. Could be blackmail. Doesn't matter. When Aurum comes knocking, someone on that ship is going to open the door. [i][The static swells.][/i] Watch for it.

signal_death.speaker: SYSTEM
signal_death.text: [i]The transmission collapses—not cut, not faded. Shredded. The encryption eats itself, leaving nothing but dead air and the faint smell of overheated circuitry.[/i]

bee_react_full.speaker: SYSTEM
bee_react_full.text: {BEE:: UNKNOWN CONTACT TERMINATED | AGITATED | lattice resonance claims require verification — crew integrity warning logged — recommend heightened monitoring of all shipboard communications}

elia_aftermath.speaker: Elia
elia_aftermath.text: [i]She stares at the dead comms panel for a long moment.[/i] Someone on the crew. That's a hell of a parting gift. Could be nothing. Could be the kind of nothing that gets us killed.

aftermath_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
aftermath_choice.text: [i]The silence on the bridge is thick enough to cut. Elia watches you, waiting.[/i]

choices.text: [Cautious] We tell no one about this. Not until we know more.
[Open] We tell the crew. If someone's compromised, transparency is our best weapon.
[Dismissive] It's paranoia bait. Classic destabilization. Ignore it.

aftermath_secret.speaker: Elia
aftermath_secret.text: Agreed. We keep this between us. I'll watch. You watch. And if anyone so much as breathes wrong near a comm terminal— [i]she draws a finger across her throat.[/i]

aftermath_open.speaker: Elia
aftermath_open.text: [i]She exhales through her teeth.[/i] Risky. If the mole is real, we just told them we know. But if it makes them slip up... fine. Your call, Captain.

aftermath_dismiss.speaker: Elia
aftermath_dismiss.text: Maybe. [i]She doesn't look convinced.[/i] But I'm sleeping with one eye open anyway. Old habit. [i]She turns and walks off the bridge without another word.[/i]

bee_react_cut.speaker: SYSTEM
bee_react_cut.text: {BEE:: transmission terminated prematurely | AGITATED | partial data captured — lattice resonance claims flagged for review — insufficient information to assess threat level}

elia_aftermath_cut.speaker: Elia
elia_aftermath_cut.text: [i]She flexes her hand, knuckles white.[/i] We cut them off before they finished. Might have been the right call. Might have cost us something. Either way—we watch our backs.
